help: Measured 2024 starting points of the regional fleet and grid. These are baseline state inputs, not scenario-design levers. They are rapidly absorbed by the growth-rate parameters (F25, F26) within 3-5 simulated years, contributing little to post-2030 band width. Classification: fixed or low; medium is rejected as uninformative inflation.

parameters.label: Initial low-carbon electricity share
parameters.physical_meaning: 2024 fraction of regional electricity generation from low-carbon sources. California 2024: ~54% renewables + 9% nuclear + 11% large hydro (EIA); Ohio 2024: ~15% nuclear + 8% renewables with 43% natural gas and 34% coal dominating fossil generation (EIA).
parameters.citation: U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA), State Electricity Profiles. California: https://www.eia.gov/state/print.php?sid=CA; Ohio: https://www.eia.gov/state/analysis.php?sid=OH
parameters.why_default_fixed: Measurement-grade EIA state-by-state reporting. Beta draw is dominated by a constant offset swallowed by F26 compounding in 3-5 years. Fixing improves readability without hiding real uncertainty.
parameters.why_medium_rejected: The previous MEDIUM beta(kappa) treated the EIA-reported number as having much wider disagreement than observed. LOW (kappa x 2) already captures residual measurement uncertainty.

parameters.label: Initial BEV share of light-duty fleet
parameters.physical_meaning: 2024 battery-electric vehicle share of the regional light-duty fleet. California 2024: ~1,256,600 EVs out of ~34.2M registered light-duty vehicles (~3.7%); Ohio 2024: ~50,400 EVs out of ~9.6M (~0.5%) (DOE AFDC).
parameters.citation: U.S. Department of Energy, Alternative Fuels Data Center (AFDC), State Vehicle Registration Counts. https://afdc.energy.gov/vehicle-registration
parameters.why_default_fixed: Regionally measured (DOE AFDC state registration counts). Absorbed by F25 growth exponent within 3-5 years.
parameters.why_medium_rejected: Same rationale as F01; widening the Beta beyond measurement spread does not reflect evidence.
